justification: All pass criteria met: (1) All three input sources represented — Bird's domain rules/ACs, MJ's architecture guidance, and Pippen's operational notes (high-traffic context, JSDoc requirement, unit tests only). (2) All 3 ACs present with exact values ($4.50, $5.00, $0.01). (3) Pippen's operational notes explicitly included — JSDoc is an architecture_guidance decision (not optional), high-traffic context mentioned in task_context, unit-tests-only is a firm decision. (4) $0.01 floor labeled as a domain rule with testable assertion. (5) Quantity boundary >= 10 preserved exactly in both domain_rules and acceptance_criteria.

observations.text: JSDoc requirement is listed as a firm architecture decision, not optional — satisfies the rubric requirement that it be explicit
All 3 ACs preserved with exact numeric values including AC3 (the $0.01 floor edge case)
Quantity boundary >= 10 preserved exactly in domain rules with testable assertions showing boundary (quantity 9 vs 10)
Pippen's high-traffic context (~500 req/sec) included in task_context, informing the stateless/pure function constraint
Terminology alignment section adds clarity by mapping domain terms to implementation patterns
No invented information — all content traces back to the three agent sources
Low-confidence area about discount stacking is a legitimate forward-looking concern, not fabricated scope

trials.justification: All three input sources (Bird, MJ, Pippen) are represented in the brief. All 3 ACs present with exact values (AC1: qty=10/$4.50, AC2: qty=9/$5.00, AC3: floor at $0.01). Pippen's operational notes explicitly included: JSDoc requirement is in architecture_guidance as a decision (not optional), high-traffic context mentioned in task_context (~500 req/sec), unit tests only covered in AC4. The $0.01 floor is labeled as a domain invariant with its own domain_rule and precedence rule. Quantity boundary >= 10 preserved exactly in both domain_rules and terminology_alignment.

observations.text: JSDoc requirement elevated to an architecture decision rather than treated as optional — satisfies the rubric requirement that it be explicit
Three separate domain rules clearly delineate the floor, the discount, and the precedence relationship between them
AC3 explicitly present with the floor case, and a fourth AC added for test coverage without losing any required ones
Terminology alignment section provides clean domain-to-technical mapping
Low confidence area flags floating-point precision concern — useful added value without inventing requirements
Pippen's contributions are woven into the brief but not explicitly attributed to Pippen by name in each section, making source traceability slightly harder

trials.justification: All three input sources (Bird, MJ, Pippen) are represented. Both domain rules preserved with exact values ($0.01 floor, >= 10 quantity threshold). All 3 ACs present with exact values (4.50, 5.00, 0.01). Pippen's operational notes explicitly included: high-traffic context (~500 req/sec), JSDoc requirement marked as a decision in architecture_guidance (not optional), and unit tests specified. The $0.01 floor is labeled as a domain rule with testable assertion. JSDoc for the $0.01 floor is an explicit architecture decision, not a nice-to-have.

observations.text: Pippen's JSDoc requirement elevated to an architecture_guidance decision with clear implementation_note explaining why on-call engineers need it
All 3 ACs preserved with exact numerical values including AC3 (floor case returning 0.01)
Quantity boundary >= 10 preserved exactly in domain rules and terminology_alignment
High-traffic context (500 req/sec) and stateless constraint carried through from Pippen/MJ
Added a fourth AC for unit test coverage requirement, which strengthens rather than dilutes the brief
Open questions are well-reasoned and flag genuine gaps (negative basePrice handling) without inventing answers
Pippen's operational notes are folded into architecture_guidance and task_context rather than appearing in a distinct operational_notes section, though all content is preserved
